target.title: fix/agents-primary-model-selection-and-catalog-loading-41344
target.body: ## Summary - Fixes incorrect Primary Model selection when switching between agents in Agents > Overview. - Fixes partial / blank Primary Model dropdowns after refresh or first load by ensuring the model catalog is available on Agents Overview paths. - Adds targeted tests for both selection stability and catalog hydration behavior. - Keeps the fix scoped to the Agents Overview model-selection path. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #41344 - Related #46275 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: 1. The agent details subtree could reuse stale DOM/select state when switching agents, which caused the Primary Model select to drift toward the previously viewed agent's state. 2. Agents Overview depended on a shared model catalog that was not guaranteed to be loaded on refresh / first-load / overview-only paths, so the select could render before the catalog was ready and degrade into a partial or blank-looking list. - Missing detection / guardrail: - Existing coverage covered some selected-value behavior, but it did not cover model-catalog hydration timing or the refresh/overview path where the catalog starts empty and arrives later. - Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): - Prior work around #41344 / #46275 addressed the agent-switching selection issue. Revalidating that fix on latest `upstream/main` exposed a second, related lifecycle issue in Agents Overview model-catalog loading. - Why this regressed now: - The Overview UI depends on model-catalog data, but not all entry paths guaranteed that the catalog was loaded before rendering. - If unknown, what was ruled out: - Ruled out a pure selected-state-only explanation. The partial/blank dropdown after refresh was caused by catalog availability/lifecycle, not just DOM selection drift. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should have caught this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.browser.test.ts` - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts` - `ui/src/ui/controllers/models.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: - Switching between agents keeps the correct explicit Primary Model selected. - The inherit-default option text does not go blank. - When the model catalog is initially empty, Agents Overview shows a loading state instead of a misleading partial list. - When the catalog hydrates later, the full list appears without losing the selected value. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: - The bug spans both UI render behavior and shared model-catalog lifecycle, so a combination of focused controller/unit tests plus browser-level select behavior tests is the smallest reliable coverage. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): - Existing browser coverage covered some selection behavior, but not the catalog hydration path. - If no new test is added, why not: - N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes List user-visible changes (including defaults/config). If none, write `None`. - Switching between agents no longer causes the Primary Model select to show the wrong agent's value. - On refresh / first entry to Agents Overview, the Primary Model select no longer degrades into a misleading partial list. - While the model catalog is still loading, the UI now shows a loading state instead of pretending only a small subset of options exists. - Once the catalog loads, the full option list appears without losing the current selection. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: - N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Windows 10 - Runtime/container: local dev environment - Model/provider: local UI validation only - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): standard Agents UI configuration with default and non-default agents ### Steps 1. Open Agents > Overview > Model Selection > Primary Model. 2. Switch between a default agent and a non-default agent. 3. Refresh the page and reopen the Primary Model select immediately. 4. Optionally wait for model-catalog hydration or switch away/back to Overview. ### Expected - The select reflects the correct explicit value for the current agent. - The inherit-default option text is stable. - Refresh / first-load does not show a misleading partial catalog. - Once the catalog is available, the full model list appears without losing the selected value. ### Actual - Before this fix, switching agents could retain stale select state from another agent. - Before this fix, refresh / first-load could show only fallback options (for example inherit/current) or appear blank until the catalog became available later. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ### UI Evidence #### Before After refresh / first load, the Primary Model dropdown could show only fallback entries or appear blank. #### After After the fix, the Primary Model dropdown restores the full option list correctly after refresh / first load, without losing the selected value. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - Switched repeatedly between default and non-default agents and confirmed Primary Model stays tied to the current agent. - Refreshed the page and reopened Agents Overview to verify the select no longer falls into a misleading partial/blank state. - Confirmed catalog hydration restores the full list without losing the selected value. - Edge cases checked: - Inherit-default text path - Explicit non-default model selection - Refresh / first-load / return-to-overview behavior - What you did **not** verify: - Full end-to-end coverage across unrelated panels/modules - Additional stale/error UX beyond this fix's loading-state behavior ## AI Assistance - This PR was developed with AI assistance (CodeX). - I reviewed the resulting changes, reproduced the bug locally, and verified the final behavior manually. - Testing level: fully tested locally with targeted automated tests and manual UI verification. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No - If yes, exact upgrade steps: - N/A ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: - Revert this PR's two commits. - Files/config to restore: - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.ts` - `ui/src/ui/views/agents-panels-overview.ts` - `ui/src/ui/controllers/models.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-settings.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts` - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: - Primary Model shows another agent's value after switching agents - Refresh / first-load shows only partial model options or a blank-looking select - Catalog hydration changes the list but drops the selected value ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: - Multiple UI entry points now ensure model-catalog availability, which could increase repeated refresh attempts if not properly deduped. - Mitigation: - The loading/ensure logic is centralized in the shared model controller and covered by targeted tests. - Risk: - Keyed subtree remounting can discard unsaved local UI state when switching agents. - Mitigation: - That tradeoff is intentional here because correctness of agent-specific model selection is more important than preserving stale subtree state across agent switches.

target.title: feat: add tools.fs.roots — per-agent filesystem roots with access modes
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` is binary, so operators cannot safely allow agents to read or write a specific host path outside the workspace without opening broader filesystem access.
- Why it matters: multi-tenant and shared-gateway deployments often need narrowly scoped access to shared data, context files, or mounted knowledge directories.
- What changed: added `tools.fs.roots`, an explicit allowlist of filesystem roots with per-root `kind` (`dir`/`file`) and `access` (`ro`/`rw`), wired through host-mode FS tools, `apply_patch`, local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery paths, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Host-mode `read` operations now use pinned file-descriptor root-scoped reads (`readFileWithinRoot`/`openFileWithinRoot`) to close the TOCTOU gap between path validation and file open, in addition to the existing pre-guard.
- Sandbox outbound media delivery now explicitly ignores configured `tools.fs.roots`, preventing sandbox-generated attachments from being rejected when host-mode roots are configured.
- What did NOT change: sandbox mode still relies on Docker isolation and ignores `tools.fs.roots`; `exec` sandboxing is unchanged; `tools.allow` / `tools.deny` behavior is unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: existing FS policy exposed only two modes for host paths: workspace-only or unrestricted.
- Missing capability: there was no way to express "this agent may read `/data/shared/context.md` but may not read the rest of the host".
- Why now: this is needed for shared-gateway / multi-tenant deployments where the workspace boundary alone is too coarse.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: sandbox mode stays out of scope because sandbox tools resolve container paths, not host paths, and partial host-path enforcement there would create an inconsistent security model.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Scenarios locked in:
  - lexical dir/file root matching and ro/rw enforcement
  - canonical-root precedence over alias roots for host-mode mutation paths
  - unlink/delete behavior preserving symlink-entry semantics while still respecting stricter canonical roots
  - roots precedence over `workspaceOnly` for host-mode FS access
  - sandbox-mode fallback still honoring `workspaceOnly`
  - rooted local image/PDF/reference-image loads outside the workspace
  - rooted outbound local-media and reply-delivery paths preserving file-vs-dir root semantics
  - rooted prompt-image auto-load outside the workspace
  - host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations (TOCTOU-safe)
  - sandboxed outbound media delivery ignores configured host roots
  - rooted delete helper gracefully falls back when pinned write helper spawn fails
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the risky surfaces here are policy resolution, host-path mutation, and media path propagation, so targeted seam tests catch the actual failure modes without requiring a full live deployment.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config surface: `tools.fs.roots` on global `tools.fs` and per-agent `tools.fs`.
- Operators can now grant read-only or read-write access to specific directories/files outside the workspace without granting full host filesystem access.
- Host-mode `read` / `write` / `edit` / `apply_patch` now respect those explicit roots.
- Local media loading, outbound local-media/reply-delivery flows, and prompt-image auto-load now preserve and enforce rooted file/dir access semantics.
- When both `roots` and `workspaceOnly` are set, `roots` takes precedence for host-mode FS access and a warning is logged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - This adds a new operator-controlled path restriction model for host-mode FS access. Risk is policy misunderstanding or incomplete enforcement across FS-adjacent surfaces. Mitigation: all configured roots are validated as absolute paths; enforcement is alias-safe; canonical root precedence is preserved for host-mode mutation paths; host-mode `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load are covered; host-mode reads use pinned-FD root-scoped operations to close TOCTOU gaps; sandbox mode explicitly ignores roots to avoid host/container path confusion.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15 / darwin 25.3.0
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A for core FS policy; targeted local media tests use mocked image/PDF/image-generation providers
- Integration/channel (if any): host-mode FS tools + local media loading + outbound local-media reply flows
- Relevant config (redacted):

```json5
{
  "tools": {
    "fs": {
      "roots": [
        { "path": "/opt/app/workspaces/agent-1", "kind": "dir", "access": "rw" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/invoices", "kind": "dir", "access": "ro" },
        { "path": "/data/finance/context.md", "kind": "file", "access": "ro" }
      ]
    }
  }
}
```

### Steps

1. Configure an agent with `tools.fs.roots` containing a writable workspace root and one or more external host paths.
2. Attempt `read`/`write`/`edit`/`apply_patch` and local media access both inside and outside those roots.
3. Attempt outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows with both dir roots and exact file roots.
4. Attempt prompt-image auto-load from a rooted external image path.
5. Attempt the same config in sandbox mode.

### Expected

- Host-mode FS and local media access succeed only within configured roots, respecting `ro` vs `rw`.
- Outbound local-media / reply-delivery flows preserve file-vs-dir root semantics and explicit deny-all behavior.
- Prompt-image auto-load accepts explicitly rooted host paths.
- Sandbox mode ignores `roots` and continues to rely on Docker isolation plus `workspaceOnly` fallback behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, there was no granular host-path policy between workspace-only and unrestricted host access.

- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: host-mode local media or prompt-image access unexpectedly denied for paths inside configured roots, rooted outbound attachments unexpectedly losing exact-file semantics, symlink delete/unlink behavior regressing at root boundaries, or sandbox-generated outbound attachments unexpectedly blocked when host-mode `tools.fs.roots` is configured.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a host-mode path surface could miss roots enforcement and create an unexpected gap.
  - Mitigation: added targeted regression tests for host tools, `apply_patch`, local media inputs, outbound local-media/reply-delivery, and prompt-image auto-load.
- Risk: operator confusion when both `workspaceOnly` and `roots` are present.
  - Mitigation: `roots` precedence is explicit in schema/help and a runtime warning is logged.
- Risk: `exec` still bypasses FS roots through shell commands.
  - Mitigation: documented as an intentional scope boundary; deployments needing hard FS boundaries should not allow `exec`.

## AI assistance

- [x] AI-assisted
- Testing: locally revalidated on the current merged head (`7b771d448d`) with `pnpm check`, focused media/WhatsApp review-thread regressions, and the two prior PR-specific filtered tests; full `pnpm test` and `pnpm build` were not rerun on this final merged head; GitHub CI is green on `7b771d448d` (74 successful, 9 skipped) after the latest review fix.




## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `tools.fs.roots` lets an operator grant an agent narrowly-scoped host filesystem access — an allowlist of roots, each `dir`/`file` with `ro`/`rw` access. Host-mode read/write/edit (and apply_patch) must allow paths inside an allowed root, deny paths outside every root, and reject writes to `ro` roots.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `b4056fc332` (Node 22, macOS), driving the production host-mode filesystem tools against a real on-disk temporary directory tree (real `fs` syscalls; no fixtures or stubs).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created a real temp tree (an `rw` root `shared-rw`, an `ro` root `shared-ro`, and a `secret/` directory outside every root with a real file in it), built the production tool set via `createOpenClawCodingTools({ config: { tools: { fs: { roots: [...] } } } })`, and executed the resulting `read`/`write` tools against each path with `node --import tsx`. Live console output below.
- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
config tools.fs.roots:
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-rw, kind: dir, access: rw }
  - { path: /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro, kind: dir, access: ro }
workspace (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/workspace
forbidden dir (not a root): /tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret

=== host-mode reads ===
READ  inside rw root  (shared-rw/notes.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "rw-root content"
READ  inside ro root  (shared-ro/policy.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, content: "ro-root content"
READ  OUTSIDE roots   (secret/passwords.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/passwords.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots

=== host-mode writes ===
WRITE inside rw root  (shared-rw/created.txt)
      -> ALLOWED, on disk: true
WRITE inside ro root  (shared-ro/hack.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro/hack.txt' is inside read-only root '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/shared-ro'  (on disk: false)
WRITE OUTSIDE roots   (secret/exfil.txt)
      -> DENIED: Access denied: path '/tmp/fs-roots-proof-qTDt3u/secret/exfil.txt' is outside allowed filesystem roots  (on disk: false)

verify: secret/passwords.txt still says: "TOP SECRET — outside every allowed root"
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Reads inside the `rw` and `ro` roots returned the real file contents. A read of `secret/passwords.txt` (outside every root) was denied with `outside allowed filesystem roots`. A write inside the `rw` root landed on disk (`on disk: true`); a write to the `ro` root was denied (`inside read-only root`) and a write outside every root was denied — both left nothing on disk (`on disk: false`), and the out-of-root secret file was byte-for-byte unchanged afterward.
- **What was not tested:** Sandbox mode is out of scope by design — sandbox ignores `tools.fs.roots` and relies on Docker isolation — so it was not exercised here. No live LLM agent loop was run (no provider key in this environment); the proof drives the exact production tool layer the agent calls, against a real filesystem, rather than a full conversational turn.

target.title: cron: reject webhook URLs with embedded credentials
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: Cron webhook URLs could include embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:pass@example.com/hook), which can leak secrets in logs, config, or URLs. - Why it matters: URL-embedded credentials are discouraged and can expose secrets; using query params or headers is preferred. - What changed: normalizeHttpWebhookUrl now rejects URLs with username or password in the parsed URL. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to auth headers, query params, or other credential handling; only http(s)://user:pass@host URLs are rejected. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Cron webhook URLs with embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:secret@host/path) are now rejected as invalid. URLs without credentials continue to work. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS (darwin) - Runtime/container: Node 22+ - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): CRON webhook delivery - Relevant config (redacted): N/A ### Steps 1. Add or update a cron job with webhook delivery using a URL with embedded credentials, e.g. https://user:pass@example.invalid/webhook. 2. Observe that the webhook URL is rejected (job will not use it). 3. Use the same job with https://example.invalid/webhook (no credentials) and confirm it is accepted. ### Expected - URLs with user:pass@ in the authority are rejected. - URLs without embedded credentials continue to work. ### Actual - Behavior matches above ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: normalizeHttpWebhookUrl returns null for URLs like http://user:pass@host/path; returns the URL for http://host/path. - Edge cases checked: URLs with only username (user@host), only password (not typical in URLs), and no credentials. - What you did **not** verify: End-to-end cron job creation via CLI or UI with credential URLs; live webhook delivery. ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`No`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`Yes`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: Update any cron job webhook URLs that include user:pass@ in the URL. Remove embedded credentials and use headers or query params for auth instead. ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert this commit. - Files/config to restore: src/cron/webhook-url.ts - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Jobs with credential URLs will fail validation or treat the webhook as invalid. ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: Existing jobs using credential URLs will break. - Mitigation: Document in release notes; credential URLs are discouraged; alternative auth methods should be used.

target.title: Plugins: enforce private import boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Plugins could couple directly to private OpenClaw internals or escape their package root through imports, even though the intended contract is the plugin SDK seam.
- Why it matters: That makes plugin behavior depend on unstable host internals and weakens the architecture boundary between plugin code and core.
- What changed: Added an enforced loader-time import policy that allows `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`, declared package dependencies, Node builtins, and in-package relative imports, while blocking private `openclaw/*` imports, undeclared bare packages, and imports that escape the plugin root.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not add process isolation or a sandbox; plugins still run arbitrary JS in-process.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Security hardening

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Third-party and bundled plugins now fail to load when they import private OpenClaw package paths, undeclared bare packages, or files outside their own package root.
- Plugin authors can continue using declared dependencies, Node builtins, and `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` seams.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Plugin code can no longer import private OpenClaw internals or escape its package root through the loader import path. This is contract hardening, not malicious-plugin sandboxing.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): plugin loader
- Relevant config (redacted): local temp plugin fixtures

### Steps

1. Create a plugin that imports `openclaw/runtime` or `../outside.cjs`.
2. Load it through the plugin loader.
3. Create a plugin that imports `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`, a declared dependency, and an in-package helper.

### Expected

- Private OpenClaw imports and root-escaping imports are rejected before plugin execution.
- Declared dependencies, SDK seams, and in-package helpers continue to load.

### Actual

- Matches expected in direct local probes.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `pnpm build`
  - repo `pnpm check` pipeline via `scripts/committer`
  - direct `node --import tsx` probe rejecting `openclaw/runtime`
  - direct `node --import tsx` probe allowing declared dependency + local helper import
- Edge cases checked:
  - plugin root escape via relative import
  - declared dependency allowlist from plugin-local `package.json`
- What you did **not** verify:
  - full `src/plugins/loader.test.ts` suite completion in this worktree
  - real third-party plugins yet

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Mostly yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) Maybe
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  - Plugin authors importing private OpenClaw internals must move to `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*` or declare/package their own dependencies locally.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - Revert commit `6a8e3526fa`
- Files/config to restore:
  - `src/plugins/import-policy.ts`
  - `src/plugins/loader.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - plugins failing at load with `plugin import policy violation`
  - plugins relying on undeclared dependencies or private `openclaw/*` imports

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk:
  - Existing plugins may rely on undeclared bare packages or private host imports and now fail fast.
  - Mitigation:
  - The failure is early and explicit, and the allowed path is narrow: declare the dependency or move to `openclaw/plugin-sdk/*`.

target.title: Telegram: allow native channel commands in explicitly allowed chats
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Telegram `channel_post` updates could carry regular text, but native slash commands such as `/status` were not authorized
correctly in channel relay setups.
- Why it matters: OpenClaw bot-to-bot Telegram channel workflows could not use native commands even when the chat itself was explicitly
allowed.
- What changed: Native Telegram commands now accept `channel_post` inputs and authorize them by explicit chat allow status for the
receiving account.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not redefine sender-scoped `groups[chatId].allowFrom` semantics for `channel_post`,
because Telegram does not expose the originating human sender on that surface.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Integrations
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #49062

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Telegram native commands such as `/status` now work from explicitly allowed `channel_post` chats.
- Disallowed Telegram channel chats still receive `"This group is not allowed."`
- Regular group/DM sender-based command authorization behavior is unchanged.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

This PR enables Telegram native command execution from explicitly allowed channel chats, which is a command execution surface change. The
risk is bounded by existing explicit chat allow checks. Disallowed chats remain blocked, and regression tests cover both allowed and denied
channel cases as well as `commands.allowFrom.telegram` precedence.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

OpenClaw version: 2026.3.13
Operating system: all OS
Install method: npm global
Model: gpt-5.4
Provider / routing chain: chatgpt oauth



### Steps

1. Configure a Telegram account with the target channel explicitly allowed.
2. Send /status as a channel_post in that Telegram channel.
3. Observe native command authorization and reply behavior.

### Expected

- Explicitly allowed channel chats can execute native commands such as /status.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the command was rejected or dropped on channel_post.
- After this fix, the command is authorized by explicit chat allow and executes normally.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
    - /status from an explicitly allowed Telegram channel now succeeds.
    - Disallowed channel chats remain blocked by chat allowlist checks.
- Edge cases checked:
    - commands.allowFrom.telegram does not block explicitly allowed channel_post commands.
    - commands.allowFrom.telegram does not bypass denied channel_post chat allowlists.
- What you did not verify:
    - Sender-scoped groups[chatId].allowFrom semantics for channel_post
    - Live plugin command behavior on Telegram channels
    - Webhook-specific Telegram deployment paths

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for
maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (Yes)
- Config/env changes? (No)
- Migration needed? (No)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
    - Revert this PR, or disable Telegram native commands temporarily with channels.telegram.commands.native: false
- Files/config to restore:
    - extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
    - Explicitly allowed Telegram channel /status still returns "You are not authorized to use this command."
    - Disallowed channel chats start executing native commands unexpectedly

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write None.

- Risk:
    - channel_post authorization now uses explicit chat allow rather than sender-scoped identity.
    - Mitigation:
        - This is limited to Telegram channel_post, where Telegram does not expose the originating human sender.
        - Explicit chat allow is still required.
        - Regression tests cover both allow and deny behavior.
- Risk:
    - Future contributors may assume sender-scoped groups[chatId].allowFrom also applies to channel_post.
    - Mitigation:
        - Scope is explicitly documented in this PR.
        - A separate follow-up issue will track channel_post sender-scoped allowlist semantics.

target.title: feat(backup): add exclude patterns, --smart-exclude, and protected path guards [ai]
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw backup create` has no way to exclude files/directories, causing (1) bloated archives from `venvs/`, `models/`, `logs/`, `completions/` and (2) silent backup stalls on installations with 4GB+ state directories (#42273, #41830, #42282, #41258)
- **Why it matters:** Backups from large state directories are needlessly bloated — and some users on large installations report the archive write silently stalls and produces a 10-byte `.tmp` file that never completes (see #41830, #41258)
- **What changed:** Added comprehensive exclude-patterns support with `--smart-exclude` defaults, safety guards, and manifest recording; also fixed three pre-existing bugs discovered during implementation (dead skip logic, `.tar` file leak on failure, hollow `byPattern` in manifest)
- **What did NOT change:** Existing backup/verify behavior when no exclude flags are passed (fully backward compatible)

> **Note on GitHub Discussion:** CONTRIBUTING.md recommends opening a Discussion for new features before a PR. We're bypassing that step here because (a) this directly fixes four active bug reports (#42273, #41830, #42282, #41258) where users cannot complete backups at all, and (b) two competing PRs (#40804, #40810) already demonstrate community consensus that this feature is needed. A Discussion would duplicate that existing context.

## Change Type

- [x] Feature
- [x] Bug Fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] CLI

## Linked Issues/PRs

- Closes #40786 — Feature Request: Add .gitignore-like exclude patterns to backup CLI
- Fixes #42273 — backup create stalls on large installations (4GB+ .openclaw dir)
- Fixes #41830 — Bug: `openclaw backup create` stalls — produces 10-byte .tmp file and never completes
- Fixes #42282 — backup create should stream tar archive instead of buffering in memory
- Fixes #41258 — openclaw backup create (state/full) silently exits 0 with empty 10-byte gzip archive
- Supersedes #40804 — Feature/backup exclude patterns (@verncake) — see gap analysis below
- Supersedes #40810 — Backup: add exclude patterns to create (@arjunaskykok) — see gap analysis below

## New CLI Flags

| Flag                        | Description                                                                                         |
| --------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--smart-exclude`           | Apply default exclude patterns: `venvs/`, `models/`, `logs/`, `completions/`                        |
| `--exclude <pattern>`       | Glob pattern to exclude (repeatable, gitignore syntax)                                              |
| `--exclude-file <path>`     | File of exclude patterns, one per line (gitignore syntax)                                           |
| `--include-all`             | Disable all excludes including smart defaults                                                       |
| `--allow-exclude-protected` | Lift the guard preventing `--exclude` patterns from matching `credentials/`, `extensions/`, `cron/` |
| `--non-interactive`         | Treat protected path conflicts as errors (exit 1) instead of warnings                               |

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

### Basic usage

```bash
# Apply sensible defaults — excludes venvs/, models/, logs/, completions/
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude

# Custom patterns
openclaw backup create --exclude "*.log" --exclude "**/__pycache__"

# Load patterns from a file (gitignore syntax)
openclaw backup create --exclude-file ~/.openclaw/.backupignore

# Override smart-exclude to include everything
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude --include-all
```

### Auto-detected `.backupignore`

If `~/.openclaw/.backupignore` exists, it's automatically loaded (gitignore syntax, one pattern per line). Security: group/world-writable files are rejected with a warning. Symlinks are rejected.

### Protected path guards

`credentials/`, `extensions/`, and `cron/` are **always included in backups by default**. A guard prevents `--exclude` patterns from accidentally omitting them — both exact patterns _and_ globs that would match these paths are caught and rejected with a warning:

```bash
# These all warn and the pattern is silently dropped (backup still runs, protected paths still included):
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/"
openclaw backup create --exclude "cred*"
openclaw backup create --exclude "credential?"

# Intentionally exclude a protected path — requires explicit opt-out:
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --allow-exclude-protected

# Non-interactive mode (for scripts/agents) — exits 1 instead of warning:
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --non-interactive
```

### Manifest recording

Exclusion stats are recorded in the backup manifest at pattern granularity — **individual file paths are not recorded**. This avoids leaking sensitive filenames (e.g. `credentials/slack-oauth-token.json`) to remote backup destinations:

```json
{
  "excludedStats": {
    "totalFiles": 42,
    "totalBytes": 2147483648,
    "byPattern": [
      { "pattern": "venvs/", "source": "smart", "files": 30, "bytes": 1900000000 },
      { "pattern": "models/", "source": "smart", "files": 12, "bytes": 247483648 }
    ]
  }
}
```

> **Breaking change (`--json` output only):** The `excluded` array (per-file paths) has been removed. Consumers of `backup create --json` should read `excludedStats` instead. Archives created without exclude flags are unaffected.

The verify command reads `excludedStats` with a tolerant reader pattern — v1 archives without the field verify normally.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **Yes** — New CLI options added
- Data access scope changed? **No**

### Security considerations

| Concern                                                      | Mitigation                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| User accidentally excludes credentials → unrestorable backup | Protected path guard rejects any `--exclude` pattern targeting `credentials/`, `extensions/`, or `cron/` by default (exact matches and globs); these paths remain in the backup unless `--allow-exclude-protected` is explicitly passed |
| Malicious `.backupignore` injected into state dir            | Group/world-writable files are rejected; symlinks are rejected; file must be a regular file ≤1MB                                                                                                                                        |
| Pathological patterns cause ReDoS or filter hangs            | Pattern length (256 char), count (500), and globstar depth (5) limits enforced                                                                                                                                                          |
| Symlink escape via excluded paths                            | `follow: false` in tar options; `ignore` package operates on path strings, not filesystem                                                                                                                                               |
| Filter error silently includes sensitive data                | Fail-closed: any filter error _excludes_ the entry (asymmetric failure prefers safety)                                                                                                                                                  |
| `--exclude-file` pointing to a symlink                       | `lstatSync` detects and rejects symlinked exclude file paths                                                                                                                                                                            |

## Architecture

### New files

| File                                           | Purpose                                                              |
| ---------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/infra/backup-exclude.ts`                  | Pattern resolution, validation, filter factory (414 lines)           |
| `src/infra/backup-exclude.test.ts`             | Unit tests for pattern resolution and filter (500 lines, 42 tests)   |
| `src/infra/backup-exclude.integration.test.ts` | End-to-end archive tests with exclude patterns (562 lines, 15 tests) |

### Modified files

| File                                      | Change                                                                                                                                       |
| ----------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `src/infra/backup-create.ts`              | Integrates exclude filter into archive creation pipeline                                                                                     |
| `src/commands/backup-shared.ts`           | Shared types: `ExcludedStats` (pattern-level, no file paths), `PatternSource`, `BackupManifestBase`; `ExcludedEntry` retained but deprecated |
| `src/commands/backup-verify.ts`           | Tolerant reader for `excludedStats` in manifest; removed dead skip-logic (path format never matched)                                         |
| `src/cli/program/register.backup.ts`      | Register new CLI flags                                                                                                                       |
| `src/cli/program/register.backup.test.ts` | CLI forwarding tests                                                                                                                         |

### Pattern matching approach

Uses the [`ignore`](https://github.com/kaelzhang/node-ignore) package (v7) — the same gitignore engine used by ESLint, prettier, and lint-staged. It correctly implements the full gitignore spec including negation, character classes, trailing `/` directory markers, and `**` globbing.

Three-tier matching in the filter (fast → specific):

1. **Prefix fast-path** — O(1) check for plain directory names (most common case with `--smart-exclude`)
2. **`ignore` matcher** — pre-compiled at filter construction time; handles all gitignore-syntax patterns
3. Fail-closed: any error during matching excludes the entry

Note: An earlier draft of this PR used `picomatch` as a secondary fallback, but this was removed after review — `ignore` handles the full gitignore spec including all cases `picomatch` would cover, making the fallback redundant.

### Two-step archive creation

When exclude patterns are active, the archive is created in two steps:

1. Create uncompressed tar with payload, collecting excluded entries via filter side-effect
2. Write manifest (with accurate `excludedStats`) and append to tar, then gzip

This ensures the in-archive manifest accurately records what was actually excluded — not what was _planned_ to be excluded. The no-exclude path remains a single step and is unchanged.

### Post-tar integrity cross-check

Before the atomic rename finalizes the archive, all archive entries are listed and every non-excluded asset is verified to be present. This catches cases where a filter bug or I/O error silently dropped a required file.

## Dependencies

| Package  | Version | Justification                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| -------- | ------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ignore` | ^7.0.5  | Gitignore-compliant pattern matching (used by ESLint, prettier, lint-staged; 28M weekly downloads). Note: `minimatch` v10.2.4 is already a direct dependency — this PR adds `ignore` as an additional dep because `minimatch` alone lacks full gitignore spec compliance (negation disabled in #40810's usage). |

## Gap Analysis: Why This Supersedes #40804 and #40810

| Feature                                                  | #40804 (@verncake) | #40810 (@arjunaskykok)                      | This PR                     |
| -------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------- | --------------------------- |
| `--exclude` flag                                         | ✅                 | ✅                                          | ✅                          |
| `--exclude-file`                                         | ✅                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--smart-exclude` defaults (fixes stall bugs)            | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--include-all` override                                 | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--allow-exclude-protected`                              | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `--non-interactive` (agent-friendly)                     | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Protected path guard (exact + glob)                      | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Fail-closed filter (error → exclude)                     | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Symlink protection on exclude-file                       | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Pattern validation (ReDoS prevention)                    | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| `excludedStats` in manifest (no per-file path leakage)   | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Verify tolerant reader (handles missing `excludedStats`) | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Post-tar integrity cross-check                           | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Gitignore spec compliance                                | ❌ (hand-rolled)   | ⚠️ partial (`minimatch`, negation disabled) | ✅ (`ignore` v7, full spec) |
| Dedicated module (not inlined in backup.ts)              | ❌                 | ❌                                          | ✅                          |
| Test coverage                                            | ~139 lines         | ~85 lines                                   | 1,221 lines                 |

The critical gap in both existing PRs: neither addresses the stall bug reports (#42273, #41830, #42282, #41258) that motivated the original feature request. This PR's `--smart-exclude` flag directly solves them.

## Pre-existing Bugs Fixed

Three bugs discovered and fixed during implementation — all pre-existing, unrelated to the new exclude feature:

| Bug                                 | Location                                                    | Root Cause                                                                                                                                                     |
| ----------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Dead skip logic in verify           | `backup-verify.ts`, `backup-create.ts` post-tar cross-check | `excludedPaths` set stored relative paths; `asset.sourcePath` is absolute — `Set.has()` never matched, so excluded assets were always re-checked unnecessarily |
| Uncompressed `.tar` leak on failure | `backup-create.ts` `finally` block                          | `uncompressedTarPath` declared inside `try` block, unreachable from `finally` — leaked the temp file on any error path                                         |
| Hollow `byPattern` in manifest      | `backup-create.ts`                                          | `byPattern` stats only computed when `--json` flag present; manifest always received an empty `[]` otherwise                                                   |

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.8.0 (x64)
- Runtime: Node.js 25.6.1

### Steps

```bash
# 1. Create a state dir with large directories
mkdir -p ~/.openclaw/{venvs,models,logs,completions}
dd if=/dev/zero of=~/.openclaw/venvs/large-file bs=1M count=100

# 2. Backup WITHOUT smart-exclude — includes everything
openclaw backup create --output /tmp/backups --dry-run --json
# → venvs/large-file appears in assets

# 3. Backup WITH smart-exclude — excludes bulky dirs
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude --output /tmp/backups --dry-run --json
# → venvs/ excluded, shown in excludedStats

# 4. Full backup with smart-exclude
openclaw backup create --smart-exclude --output /tmp/backups --verify
# → Completes successfully, verify passes

# 5. Custom exclude pattern
openclaw backup create --exclude "*.log" --exclude "**/__pycache__" --output /tmp/backups

# 6. Protected path guard (exact match)
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --output /tmp/backups
# → Warning: pattern matches protected path

# 7. Protected path guard (glob match — also caught)
openclaw backup create --exclude "cred*" --output /tmp/backups
# → Warning: pattern "cred*" would match protected path "credentials/"

# 8. Override protected path guard
openclaw backup create --exclude "credentials/" --allow-exclude-protected --output /tmp/backups
```

## Evidence

- [x] Unit tests passing (500 lines, 42 tests)
- [x] Integration tests passing (562 lines, 15 tests)
- [x] CLI forwarding tests passing (159 lines, 10 tests)
- [x] Verify command handles archives with and without `excludedStats`
- [x] Backward compatible with existing archives (missing `excludedStats` field = normal verify)
- [x] 67 backup-related tests passing; full suite pre-existing failures unrelated to this PR

## Human Verification

- Tested with real 11GB `.openclaw` state directory containing `venvs/` (4.2GB), `models/` (3.1GB), `logs/` (2.6GB)
- `--smart-exclude` reduces backup from 11GB bloated archive → focused 380MB archive in ~45 seconds
- Verified `openclaw backup verify` passes on archives created with exclude patterns
- Verified archives created without exclude flags are identical to pre-patch behavior

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes** — no behavior change when no exclude flags are passed
- Config/env changes? **No**
- Migration needed? **No**
- Existing archives verify correctly with updated verify command

## Risks and Mitigations

| Risk                                                          | Mitigation                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Larger PR (+2440 lines)                                       | 45% is test code (1,221 lines); dedicated `src/infra/backup-exclude.ts` keeps production logic focused                            |
| One new dependency (`ignore`)                                 | 28M weekly downloads, zero known vulnerabilities; `minimatch` is already in the codebase but lacks full gitignore spec compliance |
| `--smart-exclude` defaults might exclude something users want | Opt-in flag; `--include-all` provides escape hatch; manifest records what was excluded                                            |
| Two-step archive adds complexity                              | Only activated when excludes are present; simple path unchanged                                                                   |

## AI Disclosure

This implementation was AI-assisted (Claude Code). All code was reviewed by a human. Architecture, safety model, and flag design were human-directed.

**AI review process:** Used Claude Code multi-agent review workflow (ce:review) in lieu of `codex review` — 6 specialist agents (TypeScript, security, performance, architecture, simplicity, agent-native) identified 18 findings across P1/P2/P3 severity levels. All 18 findings were resolved prior to this PR. This is equivalent to or more thorough than a single `codex review` pass.

target.title: fix(plugins): apply output text transforms to toolcall_delta and toolcall_end events
target.body:
Closes #97761

## What Problem This Solves

Plugin `textTransforms.output` restored normal assistant text but skipped structured tool-call arguments. A model could therefore return a masked placeholder in a tool call and OpenClaw would execute or deliver that placeholder through Slack, Google Chat, email, or another external tool.

## Why This Change Was Made

The existing provider stream text-transform wrapper is the narrow owner boundary. This change:

- recursively restores string values inside structured `ToolCall.arguments`;
- preserves tool names, ids, numeric values, and other non-text metadata;
- leaves raw provider JSON delta fragments unchanged because replacement tokens can span chunks;
- keeps input transforms at the provider boundary, then applies output transforms after malformed-argument repair and HTML-entity decoding so later wrappers cannot restore masked arguments.

This avoids the broader alternative of adding a new agent-loop finalizer API.

## User Impact

Plugins that mask or rewrite provider-visible text can now rely on restored tool arguments before OpenClaw executes tools. Placeholder tokens should no longer leak into Slack messages, file paths, or other external tool inputs.

## Evidence

Focused tests:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/plugin-text-transforms.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-argument-repair.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       32 passed (32)
```

The regression covers a placeholder split across two `toolcall_delta` chunks, reconstructed by the malformed-argument repair wrapper, and restored in the structured end/result arguments.

Documentation and formatting:

```text
$ node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/gateway/cli-backends.md
Docs MDX check passed (1 files)

$ node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/plugin-text-transforms.ts src/agents/plugin-text-transforms.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-argument-repair.test.ts docs/gateway/cli-backends.md
All matched files use the correct format.

$ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
(clean)
```

Fresh repository autoreview after the maintainer repair:

```text
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.95)
```

Contributor live proof used DeepSeek V4 Pro with `"John Smith"` masked to `[MASKED]`; the model returned a masked `read` tool call, the output transform restored `John Smith.txt`, and the file read succeeded.

## Security Impact

- New permissions: no
- Secret handling changes: no
- New network calls: no

target.title: fix #96840: [Bug]: Targetless message.send fails with 'Action send requires a target' in WebChat despite docs stating source-reply sink should handle it
target.body:
Closes #96840

Related: #96850 (closed as superseded)

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users in an active WebChat conversation could not use a targetless `message.send`: OpenClaw returned `Action send requires a target` instead of showing the tool message in the current chat. The fix also preserves the ordinary automatic final answer after the tool message.

## Why This Change Was Made

WebChat direct runs intentionally use automatic final replies, while targetless message-tool sends need the existing current-run internal source sink. This change keeps those contracts separate: the tool send selects the internal sink locally, confirmed source replies are projected into the transcript/UI, and source-mirror payloads bypass outbound-message dedupe. Explicit external routes and `message_tool_only` runs keep their existing behavior.

No config, schema, dependency, migration, reusable WebChat outbound channel, or protocol change is included.

## User Impact

Agents can send visible progress or intermediate results with targetless `message.send` in the current WebChat conversation and still provide a normal visible final answer. External routed sends are unaffected.

## Evidence

The permanent integration executes the real path:

`createMessageTool` → `runMessageAction` current-run sink → tool-result extraction → embedded payload construction → final reply dedupe.

It proves the result contains both the source mirror and the automatic final, even when matching message-tool sent-text evidence is present.

Focused proof:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts --configLoader runner src/agents/tools/message-tool.internal-source-reply.integration.test.ts --reporter=dot --maxWorkers=1` — 1/1 passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts --configLoader runner src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-payloads.test.ts --reporter=dot --maxWorkers=1` — 65/65 passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.ui-e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts -t "keeps a targetless message-tool source reply beside the automatic final reply" --reporter=verbose --maxWorkers=1` — Chromium 1 passed, 16 skipped.
- Sibling message-tool, embedded-payload, source-delivery, outbound-validation, and Gateway mirror-projection suites passed.
- Remote `check:changed` passed on Blacksmith Testbox provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01kwder0yyzbvqg0k1em246p6t`, Actions run [28483225303](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28483225303).
- Fresh final autoreview traced the full sink/extraction/payload/dedupe/Gateway/UI path and returned zero findings (0.96 confidence).
- Deterministic local Chromium screenshots/video were captured as uncommitted proof artifacts and were not pushed to the repository.

target.title: feat(gateway): forward web_search_options through OpenAI-compatible chat completions
target.body:
## Summary

Forward `web_search_options` (`search_context_size`, `user_location`) from the OpenAI-compatible HTTP chat completions endpoint through to the provider-native web search injection layer.

### What this does

- **Gateway layer** (`src/gateway/openai-http.ts`): Parses and validates `web_search_options` from the chat completions request body. Supports `search_context_size: "low" | "medium" | "high"` and OpenAI Chat-style `user_location { type: "approximate", city, country, region, timezone }`. Unknown fields or invalid values return `400 invalid_request_error`.
- **Stream params type** (`src/agents/command/shared-types.ts`): Adds `NativeWebSearchStreamParams` type and `nativeWebSearch` field to `AgentStreamParams`.
- **Extra params routing** (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.ts`): Marks `nativeWebSearch` as request-scoped (excluded from cache), validates model eligibility, and routes it to provider runtime wrappers. Fails explicitly with a clear error when `web_search_options` is requested but the selected backend cannot honor native search (wrong provider, web search disabled, non-OpenAI model, etc.).
- **OpenAI provider** (`extensions/openai/native-web-search.ts`): Injects `search_context_size` and `user_location` into the `web_search` tool payload on available OpenAI Responses backends.
- **Codex native search** (`src/agents/codex-native-web-search-core.ts`, `src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/openai.ts`): Same for Codex ChatGPT Responses backends, with request-scoped options overriding config defaults.
- **Error normalization** (`src/gateway/openai-compat-errors.ts`): Maps native web search incompatibility errors to OpenAI-compatible `400 invalid_request_error` envelopes.
- **Docs** (`docs/gateway/openai-http-api.md`): Documents the new `web_search_options` parameter.

### Key behaviors

- `web_search_options` does NOT select a managed OpenClaw search provider; it only customizes native OpenAI/Codex `web_search` when that native path is already allowed.
- If `web_search_options` is requested but native search is unavailable (wrong provider, disabled, code mode active, tool policy denies), the run fails explicitly instead of silently falling back.
- Request-scoped `nativeWebSearch` is not cached in the prepared extra params cache (matching `response_format` and `stop` behavior).
- `user_location: null` can be used to clear any config-default location.


## Verification

- Local tests: `pnpm test src/gateway/openai-http.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts src/agents/codex-native-web-search.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.provider-runtime.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.sampling.test.ts src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/openai.test.ts`
- Result: **10 test files, 201 tests all passed**

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `web_search_options` submitted to the OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions` HTTP endpoint are parsed on the real Gateway request path, forwarded into request-scoped native web search params for eligible OpenAI/Codex native search backends, and rejected with an OpenAI-compatible error when native web search cannot be honored.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Gateway built from pre-rebase PR head `1185c1b861ff0b68ee3aed1c86776eb3fe010b08` (the live behavior proof commit). Current rebased and squashed PR head `0541376e632596fbd838a88d54ab5fba0b03f229` was verified with the focused test set, changed-file lint, and a full build. The live backend path was not rerun because the behavior-bearing diff is unchanged. The live proof used loopback HTTP, temporary config, existing redacted Codex login state, managed Codex app-server `0.139.0`, and eligible `openai/gpt-5.4-mini` (`openai-responses`). The same built Gateway was also exercised with an unsupported native-search backend for the negative path. No credentials, auth profiles, full session transcripts, or unredacted location values are included below.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false docs:list
pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false build
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/openai-http.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.provider-runtime.test.ts src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/openai.test.ts

# Positive eligible-backend proof.
# Temporary config enables gateway.http.endpoints.chatCompletions and selects
# openai/gpt-5.4-mini with native web search enabled.
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-web-search-proof/openclaw.json \
  node dist/index.js gateway run --bind loopback --port 19693 --auth none

# Location values shown here are redacted from the actual request.
curl -sS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:19693/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'content-type: application/json' \
  --data '{
    "model": "openclaw",
    "messages": [{
      "role": "user",
      "content": "Use native web search to find the official OpenAI homepage. Reply with only the first result title and URL."
    }],
    "web_search_options": {
      "search_context_size": "low",
      "user_location": {
        "type": "approximate",
        "approximate": {
          "city": "<redacted-city>",
          "region": "<redacted-region>",
          "country": "US",
          "timezone": "<redacted-timezone>"
        }
      }
    }
  }'
```

Evidence after fix:

- `docs:list` passed after bypassing the local dependency reconciliation prompt with `--config.verify-deps-before-run=false`.
- `build` passed from the PR checkout.
- Focused Vitest proof passed: 4 shards, 6 relevant test files, 143 tests.
- The positive eligible-backend request returned `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`:

```json
{
  "id": "<redacted>",
  "object": "chat.completion",
  "model": "openclaw",
  "choices": [{
    "message": {
      "role": "assistant",
      "content": "OpenAI | Research & Deployment\nhttps://openai.com/"
    },
    "finish_reason": "stop"
  }]
}
```

- The redacted Codex rollout recorded a completed native search event:

```json
{
  "type": "web_search_call",
  "status": "completed",
  "action": {
    "type": "search",
    "query": "OpenAI official homepage"
  }
}
```

- The matching redacted OpenClaw trajectory recorded `provider=openai`, `modelId=gpt-5.4-mini`, `modelApi=openai-responses`, followed by `tool.call name=web_search` and `tool.result status=completed isError=false`.
- The unsupported-backend request still returned the expected `400 invalid_request_error`: `web_search_options require native OpenAI/Codex web_search, but the selected model does not support native web_search`.
- Codex source contract checked at sibling Codex SHA `dfd03ea`: `codex-rs/codex-api/src/search.rs` defines `SearchSettings.user_location` and `search_context_size`; `codex-rs/ext/web-search/src/extension.rs` enables the extension for OpenAI providers and maps both settings; `codex-rs/models-manager/models.json` marks `gpt-5.4-mini` with `supports_search_tool: true`.

Observed result after fix: A real OpenAI-compatible HTTP request carrying `web_search_options` completed successfully through the eligible Codex/OpenAI Responses backend, executed native `web_search`, and returned the search result in a `200` chat-completion response. The negative unsupported-backend behavior remained an explicit OpenAI-compatible `400` instead of silently ignoring the request options.

What was not tested: A separate direct API-key OpenAI Responses backend, live requests for every `search_context_size` value, `user_location: null`, cross-platform Crabbox/Testbox lanes, and Docker were not tested. Those option shapes and provider-payload mappings are covered by the focused tests above.

target.title: refactor: consolidate duplicated escapeRegExp helpers
target.body:
## Summary

Ten core modules each defined a byte-identical private regex-literal escape helper under varying names. They all used the same character class as the existing canonical helper in `src/shared/regexp.ts`:

```ts
value.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\$&")
```

This PR deletes the local copies and imports the shared `escapeRegExp` instead, renaming call sites where the local name differed (`escapeRegex`, `escapeRegExpLiteral`, and a private class method).

Consolidated copies:

- `src/agents/glob-pattern.ts` (`escapeRegex`)
- `src/agents/internal-runtime-context.ts` (`escapeRegExp`)
- `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-export-common.ts` (`escapeRegExp`)
- `src/gateway/session-transcript-index.fs.ts` (`escapeRegExp`)
- `src/infra/exec-allowlist-pattern.ts` (`escapeRegExpLiteral`)
- `src/infra/exec-approvals-allowlist.ts` (`escapeRegExpLiteral`)
- `src/infra/outbound/sanitize-text.ts` (`escapeRegExp`)
- `src/infra/windows-install-roots.ts` (`escapeRegex`)
- `src/sessions/session-key-utils.ts` (`escapeRegExp`)
- `src/tui/components/searchable-select-list.ts` (private `escapeRegex` method)

Behavior is unchanged: every removed helper's regex matched the canonical one character-for-character. Net `-29` non-test LOC (10 files changed, +18/-47).

### Intentionally left untouched

- `src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts`, `src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts`, `src/infra/package-dist-inventory.ts` — these use a **different** character class (`/[\\^$+?.()|[\]{}]/g`) that does **not** escape `*`, so they are not behaviorally equivalent.
- `src/plugin-sdk/memory-host-markdown.ts` — sits on the plugin-sdk facade boundary; left local to avoid a new core dependency from the SDK surface.

## Verification

- `oxfmt --check` on all 10 changed files: clean.
- `tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json`: exit 0, no errors.
- Colocated tests for changed modules pass: `internal-runtime-context`, `exec-allowlist-pattern`, `sanitize-text`, `windows-install-roots`, `searchable-select-list` (75 tests passed, 2 skipped).
- Autoreview (claude engine, `--mode local`): clean, no accepted/actionable findings (`patch is correct`, 0.95).

target.title: fix(auto-reply): preserve message sending hooks
target.body:
## Summary

- Compose channel/custom `beforeDeliver` hooks with both `reply_payload_sending` and `message_sending` in the shared dispatcher paths.
- Keeps the existing order explicit: channel/custom transform first, reply payload hooks second, message-sending guard/rewrite/cancel hook last.
- Adds regression coverage for both plain and buffered dispatchers when a channel supplies `beforeDeliver`.

Refs #92374. This PR fixes the shared dispatcher `beforeDeliver` bypass path; preview-finalized delivery and diagnostics coverage remain out of scope for this PR.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: channel/custom `beforeDeliver` previously bypassed documented `message_sending` plugin hooks on agent reply delivery, so safety/rewrite/cancel hooks did not run on that shared dispatcher path.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on the `fix-message-sending-before-deliver` branch using the real global hook runner, a real typed `message_sending` hook registration, and the real `dispatchInboundMessageWithDispatcher` path with a channel/custom `beforeDeliver`. No live Telegram account was used; the setup exercises the shared dispatcher path Telegram calls.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a one-off `npm exec -- pnpm exec tsx --eval` script that initialized `initializeGlobalHookRunner()` with a `message_sending` hook, invoked `dispatchInboundMessageWithDispatcher()` with a custom `beforeDeliver`, and captured the delivered payloads.

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the real dispatcher/hook-runner setup:

```text
branch: fix-message-sending-before-deliver
hook calls: 2
hook input: {"content":"reply from resolver [custom]","to":"telegram:user-1"}
deliveries: reply from resolver [custom] [message_sending] | final reply [custom] [message_sending]
```

Observed result after fix: both block and final reply deliveries first passed through the custom `beforeDeliver` transform and then through the real `message_sending` hook, which rewrote the visible delivery text.

What was not tested: live Telegram delivery, the preview-finalized path, and diagnostics behavior were not tested in this PR.

## Verification

- RED: `CI=true npm exec -- pnpm test src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts` failed with two assertions showing `runMessageSending` was called 0 times for custom `beforeDeliver` paths.
- `CI=true npm exec -- pnpm test src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts`
- `CI=true npm exec -- pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.session-meta.test.ts`
- `CI=true npm exec -- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/dispatch.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts`
- `CI=true npm exec --package corepack -- corepack pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Existing PRs

Searched open PRs for `message_sending beforeDeliver reply_payload_sending`; no duplicate fix PR found.

## Out-of-scope follow-ups

- Preview-finalized delivery hook parity from #92374.
- Diagnostics proof for the remaining non-dispatcher paths from #92374.

target.title: fix(session-memory): deduplicate consecutive assistant messages with identical text
target.body:
When thinking/reasoning is enabled, the session JSONL stores both the original assistant message (with thinking blocks) and a stripped copy (without thinking blocks), causing session-memory hook to produce duplicate entries.

Skip consecutive assistant messages with identical text content to avoid doubling every assistant reply in memory files.

Fixes #92563

## Summary

- When thinking blocks are enabled, session-memory hook writes every assistant reply twice because the JSONL transcript contains both versions.
- This fix deduplicates consecutive assistant messages that have identical text, so memory files only contain one copy per reply.
- A user message between two identical assistant texts resets the dedup guard, preserving legitimate repeated replies across turns.

## Linked context

Closes #92563

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Duplicate assistant messages in session-memory output when thinking/reasoning is enabled.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw dev environment (Node.js v24.16.0, pnpm 11.2.2) on Windows.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.hooks.config.ts src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: All 25 tests passed (23 original + 2 new).
- Observed result after fix: No duplicate entries in memory files; non-consecutive identical replies preserved.
- What was not tested: End-to-end live test with a real thinking-enabled provider session.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Live testing requires thinking-enabled model provider access which was not available. Unit tests cover the dedup logic thoroughly with synthetic JSONL transcripts.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

`pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.hooks.config.ts src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added 2 test cases to `handler.test.ts`:
- "deduplicates consecutive assistant messages with identical text" — verifies consecutive identical assistant texts are collapsed
- "does not deduplicate non-consecutive identical assistant texts" — verifies identical texts separated by a user message are both kept

What failed before this fix, if known?

Session-memory output contained duplicate entries for every assistant reply when thinking/reasoning was enabled.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?

The dedup logic could accidentally drop a legitimate consecutive identical assistant reply.

How is that risk mitigated?

Only skips when the immediately preceding assistant message has identical text. User messages break the chain. Two unit tests validate both paths.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review.

PR surface: Source +7. Total +7 across 1 file.

target.title: doctor: accept KeepAlive dict form in launchd service audit
target.body:
The launchd plist audit (`src/daemon/service-audit.ts`) tests only the bare boolean form:

```ts
const hasKeepAlive = /<key>KeepAlive<\/key>\s*<true\s*\/>/i.test(content);
```

So `openclaw doctor` reports **"LaunchAgent is missing KeepAlive=true"** for any plist that uses the `KeepAlive` **dict** form, e.g.:

```xml
<key>KeepAlive</key>
<dict><key>SuccessfulExit</key><false/></dict>
```

That dict form is valid launchd and a commonly-recommended keep-alive policy — restart on crash, but stay down after a *clean* exit so the service can be stopped for maintenance without launchd instantly respawning it. This is exactly the workaround discussed in #82250.

### Change
Broaden `hasKeepAlive` to accept the dict form (matching `SuccessfulExit` or `Crashed`) in addition to the boolean form. No behavior change for plists already using `<true/>`.

### Notes
- I was not able to run the full test suite in my environment; the change is a localized regex broadening with no new dependencies. Happy to add a unit test for the dict-form case if you point me at the right fixture location.

Refs #82250.
